name: runtime_session
description_lines: Start, inspect, or stop a daemon-managed Godot runtime session.

`start` first runs scene-execution gates: C# projects run the same kind of explicit root .csproj Debug build Godot performs before Play, the scene runs structural validate_scene preflight without a headless runtime pass, and Fennara checks GDScript autoloads plus scene-attached GDScripts with targeted diagnostics.

Unlike the isolated compile-only build used by script_diagnostics scan_project:true, this runtime build updates .godot/mono/temp/bin/Debug so the launched scene uses the latest C# code. An already-open Godot editor may detect that assembly change and attempt its normal hot reload; Fennara does not close the editor.

For C# projects, start waits for any plugin-owned background C# preparation before running its authoritative runtime build. The wait occurs on a tool worker and does not block the Godot editor main thread.
If those gates fail, the scene is not opened.

Fennara currently allows one managed runtime session globally across all connected Godot editors; if another runtime session is already running, start is blocked and you must call status or stop first.
If gates pass, Fennara launches one scene from the selected/bound Godot project in a separate windowed Godot process with the runtime helper enabled.

Runtime sessions enable Godot's local debugger with error breaks ignored or auto-continued so debugger-aware addons can send debugger messages without requiring an attached editor session; normal errors and warnings are still captured in runtime_session.log.
The returned session_id can be reused for multiple runtime_script probes against the same running scene.
`start` saves one startup viewport capture under the session captures/ folder and, when the caller/model supports image input, returns that capture as image context in addition to the text receipt.
End the session with runtime_session.stop or with a final runtime_script that calls ctx.close_scene().
`status` and `stop` are operational receipts; a stopped process or non-zero exit code after an intentional stop is not by itself proof of a scene failure.
The returned runtime_session.log path is the full source of truth for scene startup output, runtime errors, runtime_script ctx.log output, capture events, and completion markers.

`start` waits up to 5 seconds after a successful spawn for FENNARA_RUNTIME_SESSION_READY and the FENNARA_RUNTIME_ORIENTATION_NOTE startup orientation tail marker before reading the startup log excerpt, so early Godot output and orientation lines usually appear in the start receipt.

If the ready marker or orientation tail marker is not seen during that window, the receipt says so; call status or read runtime_session.log to see whether the scene became ready and printed orientation afterward.
Runtime receipts include a capped excerpt of new runtime log lines when available.

Fennara tracks a per-session log cursor: runtime_session, runtime_script, status, and stop results show log lines after the last remembered line, report omitted/truncated counts when capped, then advance the cursor to the current end of the log.
Read the full raw log when older history or omitted text matters.
Runtime sessions remain live between tool calls; after any planning delay, call status or run a fresh observation probe before acting.
Stop the session as soon as runtime work is complete.
Artifacts and raw stdout/stderr logs are saved under the current tool log directory.

action.description: The runtime session operation. `status` is safe anytime and returns known log/artifact paths and whether the managed process is still running. `start` runs preflight gates and then creates a managed logged runtime session only if they pass and no other runtime session is currently running anywhere in the daemon. `stop` closes a running session when you are done or when cleanup is needed. Treat status/stop results as process receipts; for scene behavior and failure evidence, use the returned runtime log update and read the full runtime_session.log when older history or omitted text matters.

scene_path.description: Required for action `start`. The exact `res://` path to a `.tscn` scene to play.

max_run_seconds.description: Optional for action `start`; reserved for future auto-stop behavior. Stop the session explicitly when done.

session_id.description: Optional for action `status` or `stop`. The managed runtime session id returned by `runtime_session.start`. If omitted, Fennara uses the active daemon session id known to the connected editor. Pass this explicitly when recovering, stopping, or checking a session after target/project focus may have changed.
